reason: REFUTE shipping as-is. (1) Blanket blast radius with verification deferred: this prepends a domain-redefining severity ladder to ALL 20 agents at once, including the 9 currently-PASS/healthy ones (privacy, performance, application-logic, contracts, data-lineage, architecture, api-design, accessibility, observability-infra), yet the changelist defers all numeric re-derivation and verification to Phase 3 (threshold-rederivation-001). No per-agent eval gate runs before this lands. (2) Concrete under-flagging risk in a healthy agent: privacy currently scores recall 1.00 / fp 0.00 on a FLAT rule (src/framework_cli/review/agents/privacy.md: 'PII in logs or unnecessary PII collection is high', no ladder, no demonstrability gate). The rubric redefines high to require 'a concrete, demonstrable defect that WILL cause incorrect behavior... a defect that exists today on a changed line, not a speculative future failure, and not the mere absence of an optional hardening' and demotes 'defense-in-depth hardening the codebase does not itself adopt' to 'info at most'. Privacy's whole job is flagging LATENT leak risk; the privacy-001 entry asserts 'outcome unchanged' but that is an assertion, not an eval result, and the new ladder plausibly demotes PII-leak findings below the high block bar. The same risk applies to every currently-clean agent. (3) The dominant codebase-bar carve-out is factually conditional, not absolute: the rubric tells every agent that 'the framework auto-traces via FastAPIInstrumentor / SQLAlchemyInstrumentor... the absence of a manual span/metric/log on a path that runs through the instrumented seam is NOT a defect.' VERIFIED in template: configure_tracing / configure_datastore_instrumentation are BOTH gated on `if not settings.otel_enabled: return`, and settings.py.jinja sets `otel_enabled: bool = False` by DEFAULT. The auto-instrumentation seam therefore does NOT exist at runtime in a default-config project, and a reviewer reading a diff cannot see the deployment config. Instructing the agent to treat the seam as always-present will wave through a genuinely uninstrumented mutation path as 'covered by the auto-seam' whenever OTel is disabled, under-flagging the exact observability defect class Phase 1 is meant to repair. (4) The remaining FAILs that are NOT good-fixture over-flag (contracts recall 0.50, data-lineage recall 0.17, observability-db recall 0.00) are under-finding failures that this rubric does nothing to fix and, via its high-bar ladder plus scope fences (data-integrity owns transaction state, performance owns scans, privacy owns PII), can only make stricter. The corpus correctly routes those to FIXTURE replacements, but bundling them under a single P0 'highest-leverage' rubric prepend that also tightens severity risks net recall loss on already-weak agents. The factual template assertions (expire_on_commit=False, created_at server_default, items.name no UNIQUE, plain str signing secret, no SecretStr/min_length) all verify out, so the rubric is well-grounded for the over-flag cohort — but a change that redefines 'high' for 20 agents (9 of them already passing) and encodes a runtime-conditional seam as an unconditional codebase fact, with verification punted to Phase 3, should not ship as one blanket commit. DEFAULT-refuted stands.
